meta.note: Replace 'Aurora Tech Solutions' with your real company name via find/replace. Scenarios are intentionally light/funny + work-realistic so they land well in a training session.

white.name: White Hat
white.prompt: What do we actually know? What data is missing?

red.name: Red Hat
red.prompt: What's your gut reaction, no justification needed?

black.name: Black Hat
black.prompt: What could go wrong? What are the weaknesses?

yellow.name: Yellow Hat
yellow.prompt: What's the upside? Why could this work?

green.name: Green Hat
green.prompt: What's a new idea or alternative approach?

blue.name: Blue Hat
blue.prompt: How do we organize this thinking / what's the next step?

scenarios.title: The Coffee Machine Uprising
scenarios.scenario_text: The office coffee machine at Aurora Tech Solutions broke down for the third time this month, and the team WhatsApp group is on fire with 47 unread messages.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: This is the third breakdown in 30 days, and the warranty expired two weeks ago.
Honestly I'm just annoyed, I need my coffee before 9am standup.
If we buy a cheap replacement again, we'll be back here in a month.
A new machine could double as a fun team decision to vote on together.
What if we set up a rotating 'coffee captain' and a subscription bean delivery instead?
Let's get quotes from three vendors and decide by Friday's stand-up.

white.sample_answer: The machine has failed 3 times in 30 days and is out of warranty; replacement costs roughly EGP X.

red.sample_answer: People are frustrated and their mornings feel harder without reliable coffee.

black.sample_answer: A cheap fix risks repeating the same breakdown cycle and wasting more money long-term.

yellow.sample_answer: Choosing a new machine together can be a small morale win and a shared decision.

green.sample_answer: Try a bean-subscription service or a rotating 'coffee captain' role instead of another cheap machine.

blue.sample_answer: Collect 3 vendor quotes and decide by Friday's stand-up.

scenarios.title: The Mystery Lunch Thief
scenarios.scenario_text: Someone keeps eating labeled lunches from the office fridge at Aurora Tech Solutions, and the intern is the prime suspect (but denies everything).

puzzle_sentences.sentence: Three lunches have gone missing in two weeks, all between 1-2pm.
It feels disrespectful, people are genuinely upset about it.
Publicly accusing someone without proof could damage trust in the team.
This could be a fun excuse to set up a shared team snack fund instead.
What about a mini fridge camera or a funny 'wanted poster' to lighten the mood?
Let's send a friendly reminder email first before escalating.

white.sample_answer: 3 lunches disappeared over 2 weeks, always between 1-2pm.

red.sample_answer: People feel disrespected and a bit on edge about it.

black.sample_answer: Accusing without proof risks damaging trust and team morale.

yellow.sample_answer: Turn it into an opportunity: start a shared team snack fund.

green.sample_answer: A humorous 'wanted poster' or labeled fridge zones could solve it playfully.

blue.sample_answer: Send a friendly reminder first, escalate only if it continues.

scenarios.title: The Meeting That Should Have Been an Email
scenarios.scenario_text: Aurora Tech Solutions just had a 90-minute meeting to decide the color of a button on the app. Half the team was silently doing other work.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: 90 minutes were spent, and only one decision was actually made.
People felt bored and a little disrespected of their time.
This pattern could keep repeating and eat into real project time.
At least the team is now aligned and won't debate this again.
What if small decisions got a 10-minute async Slack vote instead of a meeting?
Let's set a rule: decisions under a certain size skip the meeting entirely.

white.sample_answer: 90 minutes used for a single small decision, most attendees multitasked.

red.sample_answer: People felt their time was wasted and got a bit disengaged.

black.sample_answer: If this repeats, it quietly drains hours from real project work.

yellow.sample_answer: The team is now fully aligned and won't need to revisit the decision.

green.sample_answer: Use a quick async Slack poll for small decisions instead of a live meeting.

blue.sample_answer: Set a policy: minor decisions get async votes, not meetings.

scenarios.title: The Camera-Off Mystery
scenarios.scenario_text: During Aurora Tech Solutions' daily standup, three teammates always keep their camera off and no one knows why.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: 3 out of 8 teammates have kept cameras off for the past 2 weeks.
Some team members feel a bit disconnected from those colleagues.
Forcing cameras on could make people uncomfortable or feel surveilled.
Camera-off might mean people are more focused and less anxious.
What about optional fun virtual backgrounds to make cameras-on feel lighter?
Let's just ask the team directly what's more comfortable, then set a norm.

white.sample_answer: 3 of 8 teammates have had cameras off consistently for 2 weeks.

red.sample_answer: Some teammates feel less connected to the ones with cameras off.

black.sample_answer: Mandating cameras could feel like surveillance and reduce comfort.

yellow.sample_answer: Cameras off may help some people focus better and feel less anxious.

green.sample_answer: Introduce fun optional backgrounds to make being on camera feel lighter.

blue.sample_answer: Ask the team what they prefer and agree on a simple shared norm.

scenarios.title: The Birthday Cake Budget Crisis
scenarios.scenario_text: HR at Aurora Tech Solutions just announced the monthly birthday cake budget is being cut in half.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: The cake budget dropped from 2000 to 1000 EGP per month.
A few people feel a little sad, birthdays felt special before.
Smaller cakes for a growing team could mean some people get skipped.
Saved money could go toward something the whole team enjoys more often.
What about a shared team dessert potluck once a month instead?
Let's ask HR to share the reasoning and propose one alternative plan.

white.sample_answer: Cake budget was cut from 2000 to 1000 EGP monthly.

red.sample_answer: A few people feel a bit disappointed about the change.

black.sample_answer: A shrinking budget with a growing team may mean some birthdays get skipped.

yellow.sample_answer: The saved budget could fund something the whole team benefits from more often.

green.sample_answer: Try a monthly potluck-style dessert swap instead of individual cakes.

blue.sample_answer: Ask HR for context and bring one concrete alternative proposal.

scenarios.title: The AC War
scenarios.scenario_text: Half the Aurora Tech Solutions office wants the AC colder, the other half wants it warmer, and it's becoming a daily thermostat battle.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: The thermostat has been changed an average of 6 times per day this week.
People are getting a bit irritated with each other over it.
Constant switching could damage the AC unit over time.
Solving this well could actually improve everyone's daily comfort.
What about zone-based seating by temperature preference, or personal desk fans?
Let's set one fixed temperature and offer desk fans/heaters for personal adjustment.

white.sample_answer: The thermostat is adjusted ~6 times daily this week.

red.sample_answer: There's growing mild tension between colleagues over the AC.

black.sample_answer: Frequent switching could wear out the unit faster.

yellow.sample_answer: A good fix improves daily comfort and reduces friction for everyone.

green.sample_answer: Try temperature-based seating zones or personal desk fans/heaters.

blue.sample_answer: Set one fixed thermostat temperature, offer personal fans/heaters as backup.

scenarios.title: The Feature Nobody Asked For
scenarios.scenario_text: Aurora Tech Solutions spent 6 weeks building a flashy new feature. Launch week arrives — usage is under 2%, and support tickets about a totally different bug are piling up.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: Adoption is at 1.8% after 5 days, and unrelated bug tickets rose 40%.
The team feels deflated after 6 weeks of hard work with little payoff.
Continuing to promote a low-adoption feature could waste more marketing budget.
The groundwork built for this feature could still be reused for something else.
What if we pivot the feature into an opt-in beta and redirect the team to the bug instead?
Let's pause promotion, fix the bug this sprint, and revisit the feature roadmap next quarter.

white.sample_answer: Adoption is 1.8% after launch; unrelated bug reports rose 40% in the same period.

red.sample_answer: The team feels deflated after significant unrewarded effort.

black.sample_answer: Continuing to push a low-adoption feature risks wasting more budget and time.

yellow.sample_answer: Underlying infrastructure built for the feature can be reused elsewhere.

green.sample_answer: Convert it to an opt-in beta and shift the team's focus to the urgent bug.

blue.sample_answer: Pause promotion, fix the bug now, revisit the feature next quarter.

scenarios.title: The Silent Resignation Wave
scenarios.scenario_text: Three engineers at Aurora Tech Solutions resigned within the same month, all citing 'personal reasons.' HR suspects something deeper.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: 3 resignations in 30 days is triple the company's average monthly turnover.
Remaining team members feel uneasy and are quietly worried.
If the root cause isn't found, more resignations could follow.
This is a chance to genuinely improve retention and culture.
What about anonymous stay-interviews with current engineers, not just exit interviews?
Let's run a structured retention review this month before it escalates.

white.sample_answer: 3 resignations in one month, 3x the usual monthly rate.

red.sample_answer: The remaining team feels uneasy and quietly anxious.

black.sample_answer: Without addressing the cause, the trend could spread to more employees.

yellow.sample_answer: This is a real opportunity to fix underlying culture issues.

green.sample_answer: Run anonymous 'stay interviews' with current staff, not just exit interviews.

blue.sample_answer: Launch a structured retention review this month.

scenarios.title: The Client Who Wants It Yesterday
scenarios.scenario_text: A major client of Aurora Tech Solutions is demanding a custom integration in 5 days that the team estimates needs 3 weeks.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: The realistic estimate is 3 weeks (120 hours); the client's deadline is 5 days.
The team feels pressured and a bit resentful about the unrealistic ask.
Rushing it risks shipping bugs that damage the client relationship further.
Delivering even a partial version fast could strengthen trust with this key client.
What about a phased delivery: a working MVP in 5 days, full version in 3 weeks?
Let's propose the phased plan to the client today with clear milestones.

white.sample_answer: Estimated 120 hours (3 weeks) of work vs a 5-day deadline.

red.sample_answer: The team feels pressured and somewhat resentful about the timeline.

black.sample_answer: Rushing risks bugs and further damage to the client relationship.

yellow.sample_answer: A fast partial delivery could build strong trust with a key client.

green.sample_answer: Offer a phased plan: MVP in 5 days, full integration in 3 weeks.

blue.sample_answer: Present the phased plan with clear milestones to the client today.

scenarios.title: The Cross-Team Blame Game
scenarios.scenario_text: A production bug at Aurora Tech Solutions caused downtime, and the Backend and QA teams are publicly blaming each other in Slack.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: Downtime lasted 47 minutes and affected roughly 12% of active users.
Both teams feel defensive and a little humiliated in front of everyone.
Public blame in Slack could damage cross-team trust for months.
A calm postmortem could actually strengthen collaboration between the teams.
What about a blameless postmortem template shared company-wide going forward?
Let's move the discussion off public Slack into a scheduled blameless postmortem.

white.sample_answer: 47 minutes of downtime affecting ~12% of users.

red.sample_answer: Both teams feel defensive and a bit humiliated publicly.

black.sample_answer: Public blame risks long-term damage to cross-team trust.

yellow.sample_answer: A calm postmortem can actually strengthen how the teams work together.

green.sample_answer: Introduce a standard blameless postmortem template company-wide.

blue.sample_answer: Take it off public Slack and hold a scheduled blameless postmortem.

scenarios.title: The Competitor's Copycat Move
scenarios.scenario_text: A competitor just launched a feature that looks suspiciously similar to what Aurora Tech Solutions has been quietly building for 2 months.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: The competitor launched 3 weeks before our planned release date.
The team feels frustrated and a bit deflated seeing it launched first.
Rushing our launch to 'catch up' could mean shipping something half-tested.
Our version can still win on quality, UX, or a feature they missed.
What if we differentiate with one feature they don't have instead of racing to match them?
Let's keep our original quality-tested timeline but add one differentiating feature.

white.sample_answer: Competitor launched about 3 weeks ahead of our planned release.

red.sample_answer: The team feels frustrated and a bit discouraged by being beaten to market.

black.sample_answer: Rushing to catch up risks shipping an under-tested product.

yellow.sample_answer: We can still win by shipping better quality or a feature they lack.

green.sample_answer: Add one differentiating feature instead of racing to match theirs exactly.

blue.sample_answer: Stick to the tested timeline, add one clear differentiator.

scenarios.title: The Open-Plan Noise Rebellion
scenarios.scenario_text: Since Aurora Tech Solutions moved to an open-plan office, complaints about noise during focus hours have tripled.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: Noise complaints rose from 4 to 12 per week since the office move.
Some people feel too anxious to ask colleagues to quiet down.
Ignoring it could hurt deep-work productivity and increase burnout.
Open-plan still helps spontaneous collaboration when balanced well.
What about quiet-hour blocks and a booking system for a small focus room?
Let's pilot quiet hours for 2 weeks and measure complaint volume after.

white.sample_answer: Complaints tripled from 4 to 12 per week after the move.

red.sample_answer: Some people feel too anxious to speak up about the noise.

black.sample_answer: Unaddressed noise risks hurting focus and increasing burnout.

yellow.sample_answer: Open-plan still supports spontaneous collaboration when balanced.

green.sample_answer: Introduce quiet-hour blocks and a bookable small focus room.

blue.sample_answer: Pilot quiet hours for 2 weeks and track complaint numbers.

scenarios.title: The Layoff Dilemma
scenarios.scenario_text: Aurora Tech Solutions must cut 15% of the workforce to survive the next 2 quarters. Leadership has 48 hours to decide the approach before it leaks.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: Cash runway is 5 months; a 15% cut extends it to roughly 9 months.
Leadership feels the weight of the decision and dreads the human impact.
A poorly handled layoff could trigger legal risk and destroy remaining team morale.
A transparent, fair process can preserve trust with those who stay.
What about voluntary severance offers first, before mandatory cuts?
Let's build a clear timeline: legal review, criteria, communication plan, then execution.

white.sample_answer: Runway is 5 months; cutting 15% of payroll extends it to ~9 months.

red.sample_answer: Leadership feels the emotional weight and dreads the impact on people.

black.sample_answer: Mishandling it risks legal exposure and a collapse in remaining staff morale.

yellow.sample_answer: A transparent, fair process protects trust with the employees who remain.

green.sample_answer: Offer voluntary severance packages before resorting to mandatory cuts.

blue.sample_answer: Sequence: legal review, clear criteria, communication plan, then execution.

scenarios.title: The Data We Shouldn't Have Collected
scenarios.scenario_text: Aurora Tech Solutions' engineering team discovers the app has been logging more user location data than disclosed in the privacy policy — for 8 months.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: Undisclosed location logging has been active for 8 months across ~40,000 users.
The team feels guilty and worried about user trust once this comes out.
Regulatory fines and reputational damage are both real risks if disclosed poorly.
Getting ahead of it with honesty could actually build stronger user trust long-term.
What about a public transparency report plus an opt-in data audit tool for users?
Let's involve legal immediately, then plan a transparent disclosure and fix timeline.

white.sample_answer: 8 months of undisclosed location logging affecting ~40,000 users.

red.sample_answer: The team feels guilty and anxious about the trust impact.

black.sample_answer: Regulatory fines and reputational harm are real risks if handled poorly.

yellow.sample_answer: Proactive honesty can build stronger long-term user trust.

green.sample_answer: Publish a transparency report and offer users a personal data audit tool.

blue.sample_answer: Loop in legal now, then plan a clear disclosure and remediation timeline.

scenarios.title: The AI Feature That Might Replace Jobs
scenarios.scenario_text: Aurora Tech Solutions built an AI automation feature so good it could replace the manual work of some of its own support staff. Leadership has to decide whether to ship it.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: The feature could automate roughly 60% of current support ticket volume.
Support staff feel anxious and uncertain about their future here.
Shipping it without a transition plan could badly hurt morale and trust.
Automating routine tickets frees staff for higher-value, less repetitive work.
What about reskilling affected staff into AI-oversight or customer-success roles?
Let's ship gradually with a reskilling plan announced before rollout, not after.

white.sample_answer: The feature could automate about 60% of current support tickets.

red.sample_answer: Support staff feel anxious and uncertain about job security.

black.sample_answer: Shipping without a transition plan risks serious morale and trust damage.

yellow.sample_answer: Staff can shift to higher-value, more meaningful work once routine tickets are automated.

green.sample_answer: Reskill affected staff into AI-oversight or customer-success roles.

blue.sample_answer: Roll out gradually and announce the reskilling plan before launch, not after.

scenarios.title: The Investor Ultimatum
scenarios.scenario_text: A key investor at Aurora Tech Solutions is pushing to pivot the entire product to a market the founding team believes is the wrong direction.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: The investor holds 22% equity and controls one board seat.
The founding team feels torn between loyalty to their vision and financial pressure.
Refusing outright could jeopardize the next funding round.
The investor's market data might reveal a real opportunity worth testing.
What about a small pilot in the new market while keeping the core product alive?
Let's request a joint 60-day market-validation sprint before committing either way.

white.sample_answer: The investor holds 22% equity and one board seat.

red.sample_answer: The founders feel torn between their vision and investor pressure.

black.sample_answer: Outright refusal risks damaging the relationship and future funding.

yellow.sample_answer: The investor's data could point to a real, testable opportunity.

green.sample_answer: Run a small pilot in the new market while keeping the core product running.

blue.sample_answer: Propose a joint 60-day validation sprint before a full pivot decision.

scenarios.title: The Whistleblower Email
scenarios.scenario_text: An anonymous email lands in HR's inbox at Aurora Tech Solutions, alleging a senior manager has been inflating team performance reports for a year.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: The allegation covers 12 months of reports; no evidence has been verified yet.
HR feels the seriousness and pressure of handling this discreetly and fairly.
Mishandling the investigation could expose the company to legal and morale risk either way.
A fair, thorough investigation reinforces that the company takes integrity seriously.
What about bringing in an independent third-party investigator to keep it unbiased?
Let's start a confidential, documented investigation process before any conclusions.

white.sample_answer: Allegation spans 12 months of reports; nothing verified yet.

red.sample_answer: HR feels the weight of handling this fairly and discreetly.

black.sample_answer: A mishandled investigation risks legal exposure and morale damage regardless of outcome.

yellow.sample_answer: A fair process reinforces that the company values integrity.

green.sample_answer: Bring in an independent investigator to ensure objectivity.

blue.sample_answer: Begin a confidential, well-documented investigation before drawing conclusions.

scenarios.title: The Acquisition Offer
scenarios.scenario_text: A large corporation offers to acquire Aurora Tech Solutions for a life-changing sum, but employees fear culture loss and possible role cuts post-acquisition.

puzzle_sentences.sentence: The offer values the company at 4x last year's revenue, with a 12-month retention clause for staff.
Employees feel a mix of excitement and fear about what happens to the culture they built.
Post-acquisition restructuring commonly leads to role redundancies within a year.
The deal could fund bigger ambitions and stronger resources for the team's work.
What about negotiating explicit culture and role-protection clauses into the deal?
Let's get legal and employee input before responding, then negotiate protective terms.

white.sample_answer: Offer is 4x last year's revenue with a 12-month staff retention clause.

red.sample_answer: Employees feel a mix of excitement and fear about the culture shift.

black.sample_answer: Acquisitions commonly bring restructuring and role redundancies within a year.

yellow.sample_answer: The deal could unlock resources for bigger, more ambitious work.

green.sample_answer: Negotiate explicit culture-preservation and role-protection clauses.

blue.sample_answer: Gather legal and employee input first, then negotiate protective terms.
